mnemonics: FAMILY TREE: Think of a multilevel hierarchy like a family tree (Grandparent -> Parent -> Child) to visualize the inheritance chain.
keyPoints: A class can have a parent (superclass) and also be a parent to another class (subclass).
The 'extends' keyword is used to establish an inheritance relationship between two classes.
A subclass inherits all non-private fields and methods from all levels of its ancestor classes.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to call the superclass constructor using 'super()' in the subclass constructor, which can lead to uninitialized inherited fields.
Creating overly deep or complex hierarchies that are difficult to understand and maintain, violating the 'keep it simple' principle.
Incorrectly overriding methods without maintaining the intended behavior or contract defined in the superclass, leading to logical errors.
